steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student claims that some modern southwestern irrigation methods have a lineage predating Lake Sabrina (completed 1908). The supporting quotation must show a current method whose origin goes back well before 1908.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Sprinkler irrigation systems, which were developed in the late 20th century...': originated after 1908 — does not establish a pre-1908 lineage. ✗
  • Choice B — 'In the decades following the Second World War, irrigation...increased...': discusses growth of irrigation post-1945, no connection to pre-1908 origins. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The irrigation system developed by the Hohokam people...in the 7th century CE...made use of hydraulic engineering design principles that are still utilized by today's engineers.': links a modern method directly to a 7th-century origin — over 1,200 years before 1908. ✓
  • Choice D — 'The importance of the development of irrigation infrastructure...today cannot be overstated...': speaks to current importance only, not historical lineage. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
